steps.title: Substitute x = 36
steps.content:
The notation $f(36)$ means use $x=36$ in the function $f(x)=6+\sqrt{x}$:
$$f(36)=6+\sqrt{36}$$

steps.title: Use the perfect square
steps.content:
$36$ is a perfect square, and $\sqrt{36}=6$. Therefore:
$$f(36)=6+6=12$$
The square root symbol means the nonnegative square root, so use $6$, not $-6$.
